channel.title: ThreatDigest Hub - Curated Cyber Incidents
channel.description: A curated list of recent cyber incidents, attacks, and security threats.

item.title: [DARK WEB] ThreatFox: ValleyRAT — 10 new IOCs detected
item.description:
ThreatFox Database
Indicators of Compromise (IOCs) on ThreatFox are associated with a certain malware fas. A malware sample can be associated with only one malware family. The page below gives you an overview on indicators of compromise associated with valleyrat .

Indicators of Compromise (IOCs) on ThreatFox are associated with a certain malware fas. A malware sample can be associated with only one malware family. The page below gives you an overview on indicators of compromise associated with clearfake .

item.title: OpenAI's Promptfoo Deal Plugs Agentic AI Testing Gap
item.description: OpenAI is stepping up its push to bolster the security framework surrounding its enterprise-focused AI ecosystem. Recently, the AI giant has looked to address the need for agentic AI security testing through its acquisition of agentic security testing firm Promptfoo. In a soon-to-be released interview with Infosecurity, OpenClaw’s security advisor flagged that such a security requirement existed within agentic AI development.

item.title: FortiGate Devices Exploited to Breach Networks and Steal Service Account Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to a new campaign where threat actors are abusing FortiGate Next-Generation Firewall (NGFW) appliances as entry points to breach victim networks. The activity involves the exploitation of recently disclosed security vulnerabilities or weak credentials to extract configuration files containing service account credentials and network topology information, SentinelOne said in a report published today. The security outfit said the campaign has singl...

item.title: HPE warns of critical AOS-CX flaw allowing admin password resets
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has patched multiple security vulnerabilities in the Aruba Networking AOS-CX operating system, including several authentication and code execution issues. AOS-CX is a cloud-native network operating system (NOS) developed by HPE subsidiary Aruba Networks for the company's CX-series campus and data center switch devices. The most severe security flaw today is a critical authentication bypass vulnerability (tracked as CVE-2026-23813) that attackers without privil...

item.title: The Breach Was Just the Symptom - Why AI data governance should keep board members awake at night
